we have received your letter dated January 11, 1990 seeking

siuitities 
of williaro Molinari, a Eember of the Montebello city
council, under the conflict-of-interest 
provisions of the Politi-
cal Reform Act (rtthe Actn).1
Your letter accurately reflects the advice provided to you.
Based on the facts you provided in your telephone inquiry, you
were advised that the Act did not prohibit !lr. Molinari from par-
ticipating in the council's consideration of a proposal to build a
hotel at the city-owned golf course. The parties to the Proceed-
ing regarding the hotel include individuals against whom
Mr. Molinari is currently engaged in litiqation. 
The litigation,
resulting fron ur. Molinari's unsuccessful election for council
two years ago, is unrelated to the forthconing council decision.
You were inforned that the Act's conflict-of-interest 
provisions
were not applicable because this litigation did not constitute a

night be applicable to the facts present.

sections.conclusion: Based on the facts you provided in your telephone inquiry, you were advised that the Act did not prohibit Mr. Molinari from participating in the council's consideration of a proposal to build a hotel at the city-owned golf course. The parties to the Proceeding regarding the hotel include individuals against whom Mr. Molinari is currently engaged in litigation. The litigation, resulting from Mr. Molinari's unsuccessful election for council two years ago, is unrelated to the forthcoming council decision. You were informed that the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions were not applicable because this litigation did not constitute a "financial interest" under Sections 87100 and 87103.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a member of the Montebello City Council (William Molinari) could participate in the council's consideration of a hotel proposal at a city-owned golf course when the proposal parties include individuals against whom he is currently engaged in litigation unrelated to the council decision.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC confirmed that under the Political Reform Act, Mr. Molinari was not prohibited from participating in the hotel proposal decision because the unrelated litigation with some parties to the proceeding did not constitute a "financial interest" under the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a confirmation letter regarding prior telephone advice; no separate QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections are formally delineated but the substance is clearly stated in the body text. The document references prior telephone advice dated January 11, 1990.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a member of the Montebello City Council (William Molinari) could participate in the council's consideration of a hotel proposal at a city-owned golf course when the proposal parties include individuals against whom he is currently engaged in litigation unrelated to the council decision.

CONCLUSION: Based on the facts you provided in your telephone inquiry, you were advised that the Act did not prohibit Mr. Molinari from participating in the council's consideration of a proposal to build a hotel at the city-owned golf course. The parties to the Proceeding regarding the hotel include individuals against whom Mr. Molinari is currently engaged in litigation. The litigation, resulting from Mr. Molinari's unsuccessful election for council two years ago, is unrelated to the forthcoming council decision. You were informed that the Act's conflict-of-interest provisions were not applicable because this litigation did not constitute a "financial interest" under Sections 87100 and 87103.

embedding.summary: This letter confirms telephone advice previously provided to the City Attorney of Montebello regarding whether a city council member could participate in a decision involving parties with whom he had pending unrelated litigation. The FPPC concluded the Political Reform Act's conflict-of-interest provisions did not apply because the litigation did not constitute a financial interest.
